location: 400 Bridge St, East Park, Charlevoix, MI
crs: EPSG:26916 (m), vertical NAVD88 m -> reported ft

capacity.source: no published capacity found for the pavilion or the East Park lawn — see SOURCES.md failed-search log; capacity stays UNKNOWN, not estimated
capacity.capacity_basis: UNKNOWN — no capacity or occupant-load figure is published by the City of Charlevoix for this pavilion or the East Park lawn. The city's Use Request Form asks the applicant to state an 'expected number of attendees' and sets no cap; neither the facility page nor the Use Policy states one. Deliberately left null rather than estimated from bowl area (see SOURCES.md failed-search log).

dem_type.note: PARTIALLY VERIFIED — the DEM cleanly resolves the overall bowl (continuous 11.3 ft rise, hillshade fall line matches the imagery terrace sector), but it does NOT resolve the individual terrace risers that imagery shows; terrace COUNT therefore stays inferred_imagery while rake/rise stay measured_dem.
dem_type.known_voids: (1) Round Lake is a flattened water return at 579.4 ft NAVD88. (2) CRITICAL: the pavilion structure is NOT removed from this 'bare-earth' surface — it reads as a ~6-7 ft mound (593.3 ft at the roof apex vs a 586.6 ft surrounding apron), so NO ground elevation, stage-deck height, or deck geometry under the shell may be taken from this DEM. This is the same covered-structure limitation that disqualified the Vail comparator, but here it affects only the stage footprint, not the seating.

audience_facing_az_deg.note: from OSM stage rectangle orientation + seating side; consistent w/ DEM fall line

stage_frontage_ft.note: INFERRED and weakly constrained: the pavilion roof fully occludes the performance deck in every available overhead image, so no deck edge can be digitized. Bounded ABOVE by the OSM roof circle (51 ft diameter, independent contributor digitization) and BELOW by assuming a ~5 ft roof overhang each side. NO published stage dimension exists for this venue (see SOURCES.md failed-search log) — there is no independent cross-check, and this number must not be treated as measured.

stage_depth_ft.note: INFERRED: circular structure, so depth and frontage share the same bound (the roof diameter, 51 ft). Same occlusion limit and same absence of any published spec as stage_frontage_ft; cross-check is the OSM circle only.

stage_front_to_row1_ft.note: measured 2026-07-21 from the DEM centerline profile along az 310: flat apron 586.6-586.7 ft holds to s=8-10 ft (row 1 / front of seating), continuous rise to 597.6-598.0 ft at s=66-74 ft, then a flat promenade plateau (597.4-598.1 ft) all the way out to s=164 ft. Top of seating and rim crest coincide — there is no separate rim berm.

fan_angle_deg.note: angular extent (2-98 pct) of mid-bowl contour about stage-front anchor (anchor center inferred); imagery read was 70 deg

inner_radius_ft.note: row-1 distance from stage-front arc center; contour check r(row1+2ft) = 22.1 ft

outer_radius_ft.note: top-of-seating distance on centerline; contour check r(top-2ft) = 61.5 ft

upper_row_distance_ft.note: stage front -> top seating row, on axis

row1_arc_length_ft.note: measured fan x row-1 radius (anchor center inferred)

row_count_est.value: ~5-6 terrace bands between r=12 m and r=25 m, plus open lawn and a flat promenade above; no fixed seating
row_count_est.note: inferred: counted as concentric light/dark turf bands in Esri World Imagery in the az 275-345 sector; the 1 m DEM smooths them into a continuous ramp and CANNOT resolve individual risers

ada.value: level paved plaza wraps the pavilion at the 586.6 ft apron; a paved promenade runs along the 597.6-598.0 ft rim above the seating; imagery shows paved walks flanking the terraces on both sides connecting the two levels. Whether any of these meets running-slope/cross-slope code is UNVERIFIED — no as-built or civil drawing was found.

route_concept.value: level paved apron plaza at stage level (586.6 ft) and a level paved promenade at the rim (597.6-598.0 ft), linked by paved walks flanking the terraces — both ends of the bowl reachable on paved park circulation without a dedicated ramp structure

vertical_drop_ft.value: 11.3 ft measured rise from row 1 (586.7 ft) to top of seating (598.0 ft) on the az 310 centerline

dispersion.value: plausibly the stage-level apron and the rim promenade (two elevations); no accessible-seating locations are designated in any public source found

redundancy.value: paved walks appear on both flanks of the terrace sector

sightline_preservation.value: unverified; lawn/terrace venue with no fixed seats and no published accessible-seating plan

backdrop.value: audience faces ~SE (az 130) across the pavilion; Round Lake and the municipal marina lie immediately beyond and to the left of the stage (flat water return at 579.4 ft NAVD88 begins ~25-30 m out on az 30-140), so the water reads as the stage backdrop from the seating. UNLIKE Petoskey, the band shell is a closed stone structure — it occludes the water directly behind the stage; the lake is seen past the shell's flanks, not through the stage opening.
